title: Present simple or present continuous?
explanation:
Present simple or present continuous?

Present simple — habits, facts, routines (every day, usually, always).

Present continuous — now, today, at the moment, this week.

  • I work in a shop. (fact / routine)
  • I am working now. (now)

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: I usually [*go|am going|going] to bed at ten.
They [*aren't studying|don't study|doesn't study] today — it's a holiday.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Where's Tom? He ____ a shower. | *is having | has | have
She ____ to work by bus every day. | *goes | is going | go
I ____ TV at the moment. | *am not watching | don't watch | doesn't watch
questions.feedback: At the moment → am not watching.

questions.text: My parents ____ in Scotland. | *live | are living | lives

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: She [*is reading|reads|read] a book — don't disturb her.
I [*don't eat|am not eating|not eat] meat.
We [*meet|are meeting|meets] our friends every Friday.
He [*is working|works|work] from home this week.
